title: BLE transport: SLIP encoding worst-case could write to slip_buf[SLIP_RX_BUF_SIZE]
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/ble_ipsp_transport.c
Lines: 473-497

The SLIP encoding loop doesn't check bounds before writing:

    uint8_t slip_buf[SLIP_RX_BUF_SIZE];  // Size = 200*2 + 2 = 402
    size_t slip_len = 0;

    slip_buf[slip_len++] = SLIP_END;  // slip_len = 1

    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
        switch (data[i]) {
        case SLIP_END:
            slip_buf[slip_len++] = SLIP_ESC;     // No bounds check
            slip_buf[slip_len++] = SLIP_ESC_END; // No bounds check
            break;
        ...
        }
    }
    slip_buf[slip_len++] = SLIP_END;  // No bounds check

With len = LICHEN_BLE_IPV6_MTU (200) and all bytes being SLIP_END:
- Start: 1 byte (END)
- Data: 200 * 2 = 400 bytes
- End: 1 byte (END)
- Total: 402 bytes = exactly SLIP_RX_BUF_SIZE

This is exactly at the limit, so technically safe, but there's no bounds checking. If MTU or buffer size constants drift, this will overflow.

Fix: Add bounds checking in the loop, or add a static_assert that SLIP_RX_BUF_SIZE >= LICHEN_BLE_IPV6_MTU * 2 + 2.
